mission.title: Install and Configure Milvus
mission.description: Milvus is a high-performance, cloud-native vector database designed for scalable vector approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search. It is ideal for applications that require efficient organization and retrieval of vast amounts of unstructured data. Key trade-offs include the need for proper resource allocation and management for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Milvus CLI to interact with the Milvus database.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/releases/download/v2.5.0/milvus-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf milvus-cli-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv milvus-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> Download the Milvus CLI for Linux.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create milvus-cluster --num-nodes=3 --cluster-version=1.30 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster's credentials.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials milvus-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Get credentials for the created cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the cluster are ready and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster and their status.

steps.title: Install Milvus
steps.description:
Deploy Milvus using Helm with the specified version.

```bash

helm repo add milvus https://milvus-io.github.io/milvus-helm

helm install milvus milvus/milvus --version 2.5.0 --namespace milvus --create-namespace

```

> Add the Milvus Helm repository.
> Install Milvus in the 'milvus' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for the Milvus deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/milvus-io/milvus/master/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Milvus
uninstall.description:
Remove the Milvus deployment and associated resources. This will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall milvus --namespace milvus

kubectl delete namespace milvus

```

> Uninstall the Milvus Helm release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Milvus
upgrade.description:
Backup existing data, drain nodes if necessary, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

kubectl cp milvus-pod:/path/to/data /backup/data

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade milvus milvus/milvus --version 2.6.11 --namespace milvus

kubectl get pods -n milvus

```

> Backup data from the Milvus pod.
> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the new version of Milvus.
> Verify that the Milvus pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Milvus'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Milvus pods with 'kubectl get pods -n milvus' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or adjust resource requests/limits for Milvus.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus Helm release may not have been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall Milvus using 'helm install milvus milvus/milvus --namespace milvus --create-namespace'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Milvus pod may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n milvus' for details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new vector indexing algorithms and improved query performance.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v2.4, ensure to update your indexing configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the Milvus pods running with 'kubectl get pods -n milvus' showing status 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
